title: [InstCombine] Fold patterns which uses <2N x iM> type for comparisons on <N x i2M> vector types
author: fuad1502
description:
This PR recognizes and folds v2i64 comparison patterns described in #32961. In addition, this PR generalizes the transform to comparisons on <N x i2M> using <2N x iM> comparisons.

The recognized patterns along with the transformation proof can be seen here: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/SGp4d3 (little-endian) and https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/Z_SXUJ (big-endian)

New lit tests:
- llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/fold-vni2m-cmpeq-using-v2nim-pattern.ll
- llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/fold-vni2m-cmpgt-using-v2nim-pattern.ll
- llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/fold-vni2m-cmpgt-using-v2nim-pattern-big-endian.ll

tests.test_body:
define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_wrong_shuffle_vec(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 3, i32 2, i32 1, i32 0>
  %select = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %shuffle, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  ret <4 x i32> %select
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_and(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %and = and <4 x i32> %sext, %shuffle
  ret <4 x i32> %and
}

define <8 x i32> @icmp_eq_v4i64_using_v8i32(<8 x i32> noundef %a, <8 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <8 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <8 x i1> %cmp to <8 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <8 x i32> %sext, <8 x i32> poison, <8 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2, i32 5, i32 4, i32 7, i32 6>
  %and = and <8 x i32> %sext, %shuffle
  ret <8 x i32> %and
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %select = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %shuffle, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  ret <4 x i32> %select
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_commutated_and(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %and = and <4 x i32> %shuffle, %sext
  ret <4 x i32> %and
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_sext_put_last(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i1> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %and = and <4 x i1> %shuffle, %cmp
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %and to <4 x i32>
  ret <4 x i32> %sext
}

define <6 x i32> @icmp_eq_v3i64_using_v6i32(<6 x i32> noundef %a, <6 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <6 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <6 x i1> %cmp to <6 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <6 x i32> %sext, <6 x i32> poison, <6 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2, i32 5, i32 4>
  %and = and <6 x i32> %sext, %shuffle
  ret <6 x i32> %and
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_wrong_ext_signedness(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = zext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %and = and <4 x i32> %sext, %shuffle
  ret <4 x i32> %and
}

define <8 x i16> @icmp_eq_v4i32_using_v8i16(<8 x i16> noundef %a, <8 x i16> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <8 x i16> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <8 x i1> %cmp to <8 x i16>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <8 x i16> %sext, <8 x i16> poison, <8 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2, i32 5, i32 4, i32 7, i32 6>
  %and = and <8 x i16> %sext, %shuffle
  ret <8 x i16> %and
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_wrong_and_using_select(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %select = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %shuffle, <4 x i32> splat (i32 1)
  ret <4 x i32> %select
}

define <2 x i32> @icmp_eq_v1i64_using_v2i32(<2 x i32> noundef %a, <2 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp eq <2 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <2 x i1> %cmp to <2 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <2 x i32> %sext, <2 x i32> poison, <2 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0>
  %and = and <2 x i32> %sext, %shuffle
  ret <2 x i32> %and
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_eq_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_wrong_icmp_cond_code(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %cmp = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext = sext <4 x i1> %cmp to <4 x i32>
  %shuffle = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 0, i32 3, i32 2>
  %select = select <4 x i1> %cmp, <4 x i32> %shuffle, <4 x i32> zeroinitializer
  ret <4 x i32> %select
}

target datalayout = "e"

define <4 x i32> @icmp_ugt_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_wrong_xor_mask(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %xor.a = xor <4 x i32> %a, <i32 0, i32 -2147483648, i32 -2147483648, i32 -2147483648>
  %xor.b = xor <4 x i32> %b, splat (i32 -2147483648)
  %gt = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %xor.a, %xor.b
  %sext.gt = sext <4 x i1> %gt to <4 x i32>
  %gt.0 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext.gt, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 2, i32 2>
  %gt.1 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext.gt, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 3, i32 3>
  %eq = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext.eq = sext <4 x i1> %eq to <4 x i32>
  %eq.0 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext.eq, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 3, i32 3>
  %and = and <4 x i32> %gt.0, %eq.0
  %or = or <4 x i32> %and, %gt.1
  ret <4 x i32> %or
}

target datalayout = "e"

define <2 x i32> @icmp_sgt_v1i64_using_v2i32(<2 x i32> noundef %a, <2 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %xor.a = xor <2 x i32> %a, <i32 -2147483648, i32 0>
  %xor.b = xor <2 x i32> %b, <i32 -2147483648, i32 0>
  %gt = icmp sgt <2 x i32> %xor.a, %xor.b
  %sext.gt = sext <2 x i1> %gt to <2 x i32>
  %gt.0 = shufflevector <2 x i32> %sext.gt, <2 x i32> poison, <2 x i32> zeroinitializer
  %gt.1 = shufflevector <2 x i32> %sext.gt, <2 x i32> poison, <2 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1>
  %eq = icmp eq <2 x i32> %a, %b
  %eq.0 = shufflevector <2 x i1> %eq, <2 x i1> poison, <2 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1>
  %select = select <2 x i1> %eq.0, <2 x i32> %gt.0, <2 x i32> zeroinitializer
  %or = or <2 x i32> %select, %gt.1
  ret <2 x i32> %or
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_slt_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_commutated_gt(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {

define <4 x i32> @icmp_sgt_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_sext_put_last(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %xor.a = xor <4 x i32> %a, <i32 -2147483648, i32 0, i32 -2147483648, i32 0>
  %xor.b = xor <4 x i32> %b, <i32 -2147483648, i32 0, i32 -2147483648, i32 0>
  %gt = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %xor.a, %xor.b
  %gt.0 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %gt, <4 x i1> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 2, i32 2>
  %gt.1 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %gt, <4 x i1> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 3, i32 3>
  %eq = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %eq.0 = shufflevector <4 x i1> %eq, <4 x i1> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 3, i32 3>
  %select = select <4 x i1> %eq.0, <4 x i1> %gt.0, <4 x i1> zeroinitializer
  %or = or <4 x i1> %select, %gt.1
  %sext.or = sext <4 x i1> %or to <4 x i32>
  ret <4 x i32> %sext.or
}

define <4 x i32> @icmp_sgt_v2i64_using_v4i32_for_big_endian_machine(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {
  %xor.a = xor <4 x i32> %a, <i32 0, i32 -2147483648, i32 0, i32 -2147483648>
  %xor.b = xor <4 x i32> %b, <i32 0, i32 -2147483648, i32 0, i32 -2147483648>
  %gt = icmp sgt <4 x i32> %xor.a, %xor.b
  %sext.gt = sext <4 x i1> %gt to <4 x i32>
  %gt.0 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext.gt, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 1, i32 1, i32 3, i32 3>
  %gt.1 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext.gt, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 2, i32 2>
  %eq = icmp eq <4 x i32> %a, %b
  %sext.eq = sext <4 x i1> %eq to <4 x i32>
  %eq.0 = shufflevector <4 x i32> %sext.eq, <4 x i32> poison, <4 x i32> <i32 0, i32 0, i32 2, i32 2>
  %and = and <4 x i32> %gt.0, %eq.0
  %or = or <4 x i32> %and, %gt.1
  ret <4 x i32> %or
}

define <8 x i32> @icmp_sgt_v4i64_using_v6i32(<8 x i32> noundef %a, <8 x i32> noundef %b) {

  %select = select <8 x i1> %eq.0, <8 x i32> %gt.0, <8 x i32> zeroinitializer

define <4 x i32> @icmp_sgt_v2i64_using_v4i32_with_wrong_xor_mask(<4 x i32> noundef %a, <4 x i32> noundef %b) {

  %or = or <4 x i32> %and, %gt.1
  ret <4 x i32> %or
}

comments.author: fuad1502
comments.body: Hi @RKSimon, I just want to make sure, should I handle the other comparison patterns in this PR too or is it okay to handle them in a separate PR? 🙏

comments.author: fuad1502
comments.body: Sorry, I just realized I haven't handled the `ne` comparison pattern correctly, therefore I re-marked the PR as draft. However feel free to review it now :smiley: I am sure there are many more things I haven't properly implemented 😅

comments.author: RKSimon
comments.body: VectorCombine is typically just used for cost driven vector folds, while this is a more standard simplification - InstCombine might be a better place to host it.

comments.author: RKSimon
comments.body:
> Hi @RKSimon, I just want to make sure, should I handle the other comparison patterns in this PR too or is it okay to handle them in a separate PR? 🙏

Putting in all the comparisons in a single file makes sense to me

comments.author: fuad1502
comments.body:
> VectorCombine is typically just used for cost driven vector folds, while this is a more standard simplification - InstCombine might be a better place to host it.

> Putting in all the comparisons in a single file makes sense to me

@RKSimon After looking into the `Transforms/InstCombine/` directory, I think I can implement all comparison patterns in the `InstructionCombining.cpp` file, and then plug in the functions to the relevant `visitXXX` functions in the various other files. Does that sound okay?

Also I was wondering, from your experience, does adding rarely occuring pattern checks such as this in `instcombine` will significantly effect compilation time using LLVM?

<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {undef deprecator}-->

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> VectorCombine is typically just used for cost driven vector folds, while this is a more standard simplification - InstCombine might be a better place to host it.

Is this transformation always profitable? `i2M` may be illegal on the target platform. InstCombine is very conservative in changing vector types (See comments for `InstCombinerImpl::shouldChangeType(Type *From, Type *To)`).

comments.author: fuad1502
comments.body:
> Is this transformation always profitable? `i2M` may be illegal on the target platform. InstCombine is very conservative in changing vector types (See comments for `InstCombinerImpl::shouldChangeType(Type *From, Type *To)`).

It's profitable when: the target has no SIMD support (e.g. `-mattr=-sse`), or it has an intrinsic for comparing `<N x i2M>` directly (e.g. `-mattr=sse4.2` for `icmp gt <2 x i64>` will use `_mm_cmpgt_epi64`). Otherwise, the generated code will be the same. CMIIW @RKSimon

I am not sure how we could use `shouldChangeType` here. Should we only check the element bit-width (e.g. 32 to 64)? If we instead compare the total sizes (e.g. <2 x 64> / <4 x 32> = 128), wouldn't that be useless?

comments.author: fuad1502
comments.body:
> It's profitable when: the target has no SIMD support (e.g. -mattr=-sse), or it has an intrinsic for comparing <N x i2M> directly (e.g. -mattr=sse4.2 for icmp gt <2 x i64> will use _mm_cmpgt_epi64). Otherwise, the generated code will be the same.

Overall, IMHO I still think it's profitable regardless of the target, since it simplifies the IR, better show the intention / context to the CodeGen, and possibly allow more optimizations down the pipeline. CMIIW 🙏

Outside uses shouldn't affect profitability either, since it effectively only generates a single instruction.

comments.author: dtcxzyw
comments.body:
> > VectorCombine is typically just used for cost driven vector folds, while this is a more standard simplification - InstCombine might be a better place to host it.
> 
> Is this transformation always profitable? `i2M` may be illegal on the target platform. InstCombine is very conservative in changing vector types (See comments for `InstCombinerImpl::shouldChangeType(Type *From, Type *To)`).

> Overall, IMHO I still think it's profitable regardless of the target, since it simplifies the IR, better show the intention / context to the CodeGen, and possibly allow more optimizations down the pipeline. CMIIW 🙏
Outside uses shouldn't affect profitability either, since it effectively only generates a single instruction.

@RKSimon Any comments?
